mechanics.name: Ability-Driven Backtracking
mechanics.summary: Previously inaccessible areas become reachable after acquiring new abilities, encouraging revisits and deeper exploration.
mechanics.problem_solved: Rewards exploration and makes the world feel interconnected.

mechanics.name: Ability-Gated Exploration
mechanics.summary: A progression structure where specific areas remain inaccessible until players acquire designated abilities or equipment, creating spatial puzzles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structures open-world pacing and ability teaching through gating.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Linear Stage Gauntlet is core to the experience

games: Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy
The Legend of Zelda: The Minish Cap

mechanics.name: Ability Loadout Switching
mechanics.summary: A combat system mechanic in which the player maintains a set of available weapons or abilities and can deliberately switch between them at will, adapting loadout to the current encounter.
mechanics.problem_solved: Enables tactical variety without overwhelming the player with simultaneous options.

mechanics.name: Active Time Battle
mechanics.summary: Combat commands execute on timers rather than strict alternating turns, creating urgency during menu selection.
mechanics.problem_solved: Hybrid of real-time tension and turn-based tactics.

mechanics.name: Aeon Flux Puzzle Chains
mechanics.summary: Non-linear escape-room puzzles branch across multiple endings based on player choices.
mechanics.problem_solved: Narrative puzzle structure in visual novel thrillers.

mechanics.name: Aerial Combat Traversal
mechanics.summary: Flight and aerial dodging define combat arenas with vertical freedom.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct 3D shooter feel with altitude as a core axis.

mechanics.name: Aim Down Sights Shooting
mechanics.summary: Zoomed aim mode trades movement speed for accuracy and range.
mechanics.problem_solved: Risk-reward aiming in FPS and TPS combat.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Instant Fire Attack is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Arcade Cabinet Timing
mechanics.summary: Lean-into-turn mechanics and speed gates reward precise timing on a fixed course.
mechanics.problem_solved: Physical arcade racing feel translated to digital controls.

mechanics.name: Arena Fighter Combos
mechanics.summary: Dial-a-combo strings and cinematic supers in enclosed arenas.
mechanics.problem_solved: Accessible combo expression in licensed arena fighters.

mechanics.name: Assist Mode Accessibility
mechanics.summary: Optional modifiers reduce difficulty spikes for players who want narrative focus.
mechanics.problem_solved: Inclusive difficulty tuning in precision platformers.

mechanics.name: Asymmetric Multiplayer
mechanics.summary: Players have different roles, abilities, or win conditions in the same match.
mechanics.problem_solved: Social tension and replayability in multiplayer experiences.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Symmetric Competitive Duel is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Base Management Recruitment
mechanics.summary: Recruit and assign characters to castle or base roles that affect war outcomes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Strategic roster building beyond party combat in epic RPGs.

mechanics.name: Battle Royale Zone Shrink
mechanics.summary: Playable area contracts over time forcing squads into escalating firefights.
mechanics.problem_solved: Pacing and forced confrontation in last-player-standing shooters.

mechanics.name: Bench Checkpoint
mechanics.summary: Designated rest points save progress, restore health or resources, and set respawn locations on death.
mechanics.problem_solved: Clear save/heal rhythm and reduced frustration in large interconnected worlds.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Life-Based Retry System is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Block Placement Tetromino
mechanics.summary: Fall or place tetromino-shaped blocks to clear lines or build structures.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spatial planning under time pressure.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Real-Time Action Combat is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Boss-Gated Abilities
mechanics.summary: A progression structure in which the player earns a new, persistent ability as a direct reward for defeating a specific boss, expanding reachable content.
mechanics.problem_solved: Ties combat mastery to meaningful permanent progression.

mechanics.name: Boss-Gated Progression
mechanics.summary: A progression structure in which access to subsequent content areas, abilities, or game states is conditionally locked behind the defeat of a designated boss.
mechanics.problem_solved: Paces content release and marks major progression milestones.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Skill-Gated Only Progression is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Boss Rush Progression
mechanics.summary: Core content consists of sequential or selectable boss encounters rather than continuous level traversal.
mechanics.problem_solved: Focuses design and player skill on memorable set-piece fights.

mechanics.name: Boss Weakness Network
mechanics.summary: A systemic relationship in which each boss possesses a specific vulnerability that can only be efficiently exploited using an ability obtained by defeating another boss.
mechanics.problem_solved: Creates interdependent boss order strategy and rewards knowledge of the weakness cycle.

mechanics.name: Branching Investigation Scenes
mechanics.summary: Crime scenes and interviews branch based on evidence found and dialogue choices made.
mechanics.problem_solved: Non-linear detective progression with player-driven discovery order.

mechanics.name: Brave Default Resource
mechanics.summary: Bank turns into multi-actions (Brave) or skip turns to store points (Default).
mechanics.problem_solved: Risk-reward tempo control in turn-based JRPG combat.

mechanics.name: Building Fortification Combat
mechanics.summary: Harvest materials and erect structures mid-fight for cover and verticality.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player-built terrain in competitive battle royale shooters.

mechanics.name: Card Draw Hand Management
mechanics.summary: Players draw from a deck into a limited hand and discard or exhaust cards each turn.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tempo and resource pacing in card-based games.

mechanics.name: Character Swap Abilities
mechanics.summary: Multiple playable characters with distinct abilities must be switched mid-level to overcome gated obstacles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Puzzle-platforming depth through roster management.

mechanics.name: Character Switch Combos
mechanics.summary: Swap between heroes mid-fight to chain elemental or role synergies.
mechanics.problem_solved: Party variety in real-time action without full co-op.

mechanics.name: City Building Economy
mechanics.summary: Zone districts, manage budgets, and satisfy citizen needs over campaigns.
mechanics.problem_solved: Macro planning loops in historical city builders.

mechanics.name: City Building Simulation
mechanics.summary: Zone land, lay infrastructure, and balance budgets to grow a functioning city.
mechanics.problem_solved: Macro planning and systems balancing in simulation games.

mechanics.name: City Trial Exploration
mechanics.summary: Open sandbox sessions gather power-ups before a final showdown.
mechanics.problem_solved: Hybrid exploration and party race finales.

mechanics.name: Co-op Couch Chaos
mechanics.summary: Local multiplayer where friendly interference is as important as cooperation.
mechanics.problem_solved: Social party gameplay on a shared screen.

mechanics.name: Co-op Horde Survival
mechanics.summary: Teams hold out against escalating enemy waves to reach extraction.
mechanics.problem_solved: Social survival tension in zombie and horde shooters.

mechanics.name: Collectathon Token Gating
mechanics.summary: Collectible tokens such as stars, jiggies, or eggs gate access to new areas and stage completion.
mechanics.problem_solved: Exploration incentive and measurable progression in open 3D worlds.

games: Billy Hatcher and the Giant Egg
Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy
Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus
The Legend of Zelda: The Minish Cap

mechanics.name: Collection Completion Pokédex
mechanics.summary: Catalog and collect a full roster of discoverable entities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Long-term completion goals in creature-collection games.

mechanics.name: Colossus Climb Combat
mechanics.summary: Scale massive foes to reach weak points while maintaining grip and stamina.
mechanics.problem_solved: Boss fights as environmental climbing puzzles.

mechanics.name: Combo Chain System
mechanics.summary: Attacks can be linked in sequence or canceled into one another within timing windows to deal extended damage.
mechanics.problem_solved: Depth and skill ceiling in combat through attack linking.

games: Street Fighter II: The World Warrior

mechanics.name: Combo Trick Scoring
mechanics.summary: Chained tricks and aerial maneuvers multiply score within a combo timer during skating or stunt gameplay.
mechanics.problem_solved: Skill expression and score chasing in stunt sports games.

mechanics.name: Companion Possession Control
mechanics.summary: The player temporarily possesses enemies or objects to use their unique abilities for traversal and combat.
mechanics.problem_solved: Ability variety without permanent unlock bloat.

mechanics.name: Copy Ability Absorption
mechanics.summary: Defeating enemies grants temporary powers that change movement and attacks.
mechanics.problem_solved: Variety and experimentation in character action platformers.

mechanics.name: Counter-Attack Parry Melee
mechanics.summary: Timed blocks and counters define high-skill melee encounters against agile foes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Skill-based melee in action-adventure remakes.

mechanics.name: Courtroom Cross-Examination
mechanics.summary: Players press witness testimony and present evidence to expose contradictions in court.
mechanics.problem_solved: Detective-adventure gameplay loop with structured dramatic reveals.

mechanics.name: Cover-Based Positioning
mechanics.summary: Units gain defensive bonuses from terrain cover and flanking angles on a tactical grid.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spatial tactics beyond raw stats in turn-based combat.

mechanics.name: Cover-Based Shooting
mechanics.summary: Snap to cover, blind-fire, and flank in third-person gunfights.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tactical shooting in narrative action games.

mechanics.name: Cover Shooter Mechanics
mechanics.summary: Combat revolves around moving between cover and blind-firing or popping out to shoot.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tactical pacing in third-person shooters.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Unrestricted Primary Fire is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Crafting Economy Loop
mechanics.summary: Gather materials, craft items, and sell or use them to upgrade home and tools.
mechanics.problem_solved: Self-directed goals in open-ended simulation games.

mechanics.name: Daily Routine Simulation
mechanics.summary: In-game days cycle through fishing, farming, socializing, and shop hours.
mechanics.problem_solved: Relaxed progression loop in life simulation games.

mechanics.name: Day Night Schedule Loop
mechanics.summary: Calendar days cycle with timed activities, shops, and events.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structure and urgency in farming sims and social RPGs.

mechanics.name: Deck Composition Roguelike
mechanics.summary: Players build a run-specific deck by adding and removing cards between encounters.
mechanics.problem_solved: Run variety and buildcraft depth in roguelike deckbuilders.

mechanics.name: Decoupled Aim Control
mechanics.summary: A shooting mechanic where the player's attack vector is controlled independently from the character's movement direction or body orientation.
mechanics.problem_solved: Enables shooting while moving in any direction, typical of twin-stick and top-down shooters.
anti_patterns: Avoid when facing-locked-shooting is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Depletable Health Pool
mechanics.summary: A survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.
mechanics.problem_solved: Creates tension and punishes mistakes during combat and traversal.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Single-Hit Death is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Dialogue Choice Consequences
mechanics.summary: Conversation branches alter relationships, story flags, and available quests.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player agency in narrative-heavy games without combat focus.

mechanics.name: Dice Party Combat
mechanics.summary: Roll and assign dice faces to party abilities each turn.
mechanics.problem_solved: Roguelike build variety through die-face loadouts.

mechanics.name: Difficulty Accessibility Mode
mechanics.summary: An optional reduced-difficulty setting lowers enemy health, simplifies patterns, or grants extra resources for players who want to experience content without full challenge.
mechanics.problem_solved: Broadens audience reach while preserving optional hard mode for skilled players.

mechanics.name: Directional Burst Movement
mechanics.summary: A mobility mechanic in which the player character executes a short, instantaneous displacement in a chosen direction, bypassing normal movement physics temporarily.
mechanics.problem_solved: Emergency evasion and repositioning during dense projectile patterns.

mechanics.name: Director AI Pacing
mechanics.summary: Dynamic systems adjust spawn intensity based on player stress and performance.
mechanics.problem_solved: Adaptive difficulty in co-op horde modes.

mechanics.name: Disguise Infiltration
mechanics.summary: Blend in with outfits and social behaviors to access restricted areas.
mechanics.problem_solved: Social stealth beyond binary visibility cones.

mechanics.name: Document Inspection Stamping
mechanics.summary: Approve or deny paperwork by cross-checking rules under time and moral pressure.
mechanics.problem_solved: Bureaucratic moral-choice gameplay in narrative indies.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Real-Time Action Combat is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Dual Layer Air Ground Targeting
mechanics.summary: The player must engage airborne and ground targets using distinct weapons or attack modes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Layered threat management and weapon selection tension.

mechanics.name: Dual Screen Combat Sync
mechanics.summary: Simultaneous inputs on two screens combine partner attacks in real time.
mechanics.problem_solved: Handheld-specific multi-character combat design.

mechanics.name: Dual-Screen Interface
mechanics.summary: Bottom touch screen handles maps, inventory, or puzzles while top screen shows action.
mechanics.problem_solved: DS/3DS form-factor UI that keeps action uncluttered.

mechanics.name: Dungeon Crawl Exploration
mechanics.summary: Procedural or fixed multi-floor dungeons with resource attrition between exits.
mechanics.problem_solved: Risk-reward dungeon delving in JRPGs.

mechanics.name: Egg Roll Puzzle Platforming
mechanics.summary: Rolling large eggs through terrain solves puzzles and opens paths while navigating 3D stages.
mechanics.problem_solved: Physics-based objective layer on platforming traversal.

games: Billy Hatcher and the Giant Egg

mechanics.name: Encounter Containment
mechanics.summary: A room-state mechanic that enforces spatial boundaries around the player for the duration of a triggered event, preventing exit until the event resolves.
mechanics.problem_solved: Ensures players engage with combat or puzzle content fully.

mechanics.name: Environmental Hazard Navigation
mechanics.summary: A traversal mechanic in which the level space contains persistent damaging or lethal obstacles that the player must avoid through precise movement.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tests maneuvering skill and creates platforming challenge without enemies.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Flat Safe Levels is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Environmental Trigger Switch
mechanics.summary: A mechanic where the player interacts with a fixed element in the environment to produce a state change such as opening a door or activating a bridge.
mechanics.problem_solved: Connects exploration to world reconfiguration and puzzle solving.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Always-Open Areas is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Equipment Loadout
mechanics.summary: Weapons, armor, and accessories occupy distinct equipment slots and modify combat stats and abilities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Build customization through gear choices rather than ability unlocks alone.
anti_patterns: Avoid when No Loadout Variety is core to the experience

games: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

mechanics.name: Escalating Drop Speed
mechanics.summary: The rate at which pieces fall increases over time or level, compressing decision windows as the session progresses.
mechanics.problem_solved: Difficulty curve and tension escalation in endless puzzle modes.

mechanics.name: Evidence Deduction Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Players collect clues and deduce correct answers from constrained evidence sets.
mechanics.problem_solved: Detective fantasy and logical payoff in narrative puzzles.

games: The Return of the Obra Dinn

mechanics.name: Evolution Body Modification
mechanics.summary: Consume DNA or resources to mutate stats, limbs, and abilities between battles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Buildcrafting through biological transformation in RPGs.

mechanics.name: Exergaming Rep Tracking
mechanics.summary: Physical exercises are counted and gamified through RPG progression.
mechanics.problem_solved: Fitness motivation through game rewards.

mechanics.name: Exploration-Gated Upgrades
mechanics.summary: The character unlocks permanent upgrades obtained through spatial exploration and world interaction, rather than direct combat rewards.
mechanics.problem_solved: Rewards curiosity and thorough map exploration.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Linear Progression is core to the experience

games: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

mechanics.name: Facing-Locked Shooting
mechanics.summary: A shooting mechanic where weapon trajectory is directly coupled to character orientation, requiring players to rotate or reorient the entire avatar to change aim direction.
mechanics.problem_solved: Forces vertical engagement through movement rather than free aim, defining Jump'n'Shoot identity.
anti_patterns: Avoid when decoupled-aim-control is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Farming Crop Cycle
mechanics.summary: Plant, water, and harvest crops on daily cycles tied to in-game seasons.
mechanics.problem_solved: Relaxing progression loops in farming life sims.

mechanics.name: Fashion Stat Build
mechanics.summary: Equip pins and clothing that alter stats and ability loadouts.
mechanics.problem_solved: Buildcrafting through wearable gear in action RPGs.

mechanics.name: Fill-Reward Progression Loop
mechanics.summary: A progression gate where a fixed collection target must be met before an upgrade or skill point becomes available, tying advancement directly to measurable progress.
mechanics.problem_solved: Clear short-term goals and visible progression feedback.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Flat Progression is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Finishing Move Execution
mechanics.summary: Special input sequences at low health trigger cinematic finishers that end encounters dramatically.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spectacle and mastery reward in fighting games.

mechanics.name: First-Person Dungeon Grid
mechanics.summary: Movement occurs on a discrete grid viewed from a first-person perspective.
mechanics.problem_solved: Classic dungeon crawler navigation and mapping challenge.

mechanics.name: First Person Platforming
mechanics.summary: Platforming challenges are presented from a first-person camera, demanding spatial judgment without a visible avatar.
mechanics.problem_solved: Unique traversal challenge and immersion in 3D spaces.

mechanics.name: Fixed Formation Shooter
mechanics.summary: Enemies advance in predictable rows or grids toward the player, who must eliminate them before they reach a fail line.
mechanics.problem_solved: Readable threat patterns and classic arcade scoring loops.

mechanics.name: Frame Punish Engagement
mechanics.summary: Attacks have startup, active, and recovery frames; unsafe moves can be punished with faster counter attacks during recovery windows.
mechanics.problem_solved: Depth in competitive play through risk-reward on move selection.

mechanics.name: Free Camera 3D Movement
mechanics.summary: The player navigates a 3D space with full directional movement and an adjustable or follow camera.
mechanics.problem_solved: Foundation for 3D platforming and action exploration.

mechanics.name: Free Flight Space Combat
mechanics.summary: Six-degrees-of-freedom movement in open space with inertia and targeting.
mechanics.problem_solved: Authentic space dogfighting and exploration feel.

mechanics.name: Ghost Vacuum Combat
mechanics.summary: Suck, stun, and slam ghosts with a vacuum device rather than traditional weapons.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct combat identity for light horror action games.

mechanics.name: Glide Traversal
mechanics.summary: A sustained aerial movement mode allows controlled descent or limited flight, extending horizontal and vertical reach.
mechanics.problem_solved: Opens secret areas and softens platforming difficulty when mastered.

mechanics.name: Grid Deduction Mines
mechanics.summary: Infer mine locations from numeric adjacency clues on a hidden grid.
mechanics.problem_solved: Pure logic deduction in classic puzzle games.

mechanics.name: Grid Placement Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Player positions discrete shapes within a fixed grid, optimizing placement to achieve row or pattern completion goals.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spatial reasoning challenge with clear grid constraints.

mechanics.name: Guest Satisfaction Metrics
mechanics.summary: Monitor nausea, boredom, and hunger meters to tune park layouts.
mechanics.problem_solved: Feedback-driven optimization in management sims.

mechanics.name: Gulag Revival Duel
mechanics.summary: Eliminated players fight a 1v1 for a single redeploy back into the match.
mechanics.problem_solved: Second-chance tension without unlimited respawns in battle royale.

mechanics.name: Hard Drop Placement
mechanics.summary: An instant-drop input locks the current piece at its horizontal position immediately.
mechanics.problem_solved: Speed optimization and precise final placement under time pressure.

mechanics.name: Health Capacity Upgrade
mechanics.summary: Permanent pickups or purchases extend the maximum health pool beyond the starting value.
mechanics.problem_solved: Rewards exploration and provides survivability scaling across a campaign.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Fixed Health Pool is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Height Plane Shooting
mechanics.summary: Combat requires judging vertical altitude relative to enemies and obstacles on a separate height plane from horizontal movement.
mechanics.problem_solved: Adds depth-axis challenge to pseudo-3D shooters like Zaxxon.

mechanics.name: Hub World Stage Gates
mechanics.summary: A central hub area connects to discrete stages unlocked by collecting tokens or defeating bosses.
mechanics.problem_solved: Non-linear exploration structure in 3D platformers and adventures.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Pure Linear Gauntlet is core to the experience

games: Billy Hatcher and the Giant Egg
Jak and Daxter: The Precursor Legacy
Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus
The Legend of Zelda: The Minish Cap

mechanics.name: Humor Destructible Environment
mechanics.summary: Comedic set pieces break apart as players smash collectibles and props.
mechanics.problem_solved: Family-friendly chaos in licensed action-adventure games.

mechanics.name: Input-Aligned Movement
mechanics.summary: Movement responds directly and immediately to input direction with minimal inertia, optimized for top-down or free-aim combat spaces.
mechanics.problem_solved: Precise dodging and positioning in projectile-dense environments.
anti_patterns: Avoid when precision-platforming is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Instant-Fire Attack
mechanics.summary: The character executes an attack immediately upon input, with no charge, delay or conditional cost.
mechanics.problem_solved: Keeps combat responsive and readable during platforming.
anti_patterns: Avoid when time-charged-attack is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Insult Sword Fighting
mechanics.summary: Verbal retort duels use learned insults and comebacks as combat resolution.
mechanics.problem_solved: Comedic alternative combat in adventure games.

mechanics.name: Insurance Investigation Deduction
mechanics.summary: Reconstruct incidents from witness accounts and scene evidence.
mechanics.problem_solved: Logical deduction gameplay in narrative mysteries.

games: The Return of the Obra Dinn

mechanics.name: Investigation Dungeon Loop
mechanics.summary: Social days gather clues; dungeon nights apply them to unlock new floors.
mechanics.problem_solved: Alternating daily life and dungeon pacing in Persona-style games.

mechanics.name: Job System Combat
mechanics.summary: Characters switch between jobs/classes mid-battle with distinct ability sets.
mechanics.problem_solved: Flexible party builds and combat adaptability in JRPGs.

mechanics.name: Jump Cutoff
mechanics.summary: A jump system where releasing the jump input early triggers an immediate reduction of upward momentum, causing shorter hops and precise vertical control.
mechanics.problem_solved: Enables fine platforming control while shooting; hallmark of Jump'n'Shoot when paired with facing-locked aim.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Fixed Jump Arc Only is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Kart Item Race Combat
mechanics.summary: Rubber-banding item boxes provide offensive and defensive tools during circuit races.
mechanics.problem_solved: Chaotic comeback mechanics in kart racers.

mechanics.name: Key Collect Rescue Objective
mechanics.summary: The player must collect keys or items sequentially and deliver them to a rescue target to advance.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structures multi-step goals within single-screen or short stages.

mechanics.name: Lane Push MOBA
mechanics.summary: Teams push minion waves along lanes toward enemy structures.
mechanics.problem_solved: Macro map control and team objective pressure.

mechanics.name: Last Hit Farming
mechanics.summary: Gold or XP rewards require dealing the killing blow to creeps or objectives.
mechanics.problem_solved: Skill expression and economy pacing in lane-based games.

mechanics.name: License Progression Gates
mechanics.summary: Structured license tests or certifications unlock higher vehicle tiers and race categories.
mechanics.problem_solved: Guided skill progression in racing simulators.

mechanics.name: Life-Based Retry System
mechanics.summary: A failure-buffer system in which the player is granted a limited number of retries, losing one life upon failure and restarting from a previous checkpoint.
mechanics.problem_solved: Softens failure while maintaining stakes through limited retries per level.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Bench Checkpoint is core to the experience
Avoid when Mid-Level Checkpoint is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Line Clear Scoring
mechanics.summary: Completing full horizontal rows in a grid clears them and awards points, often with bonuses for multiple simultaneous clears.
mechanics.problem_solved: Core goal and scoring loop for falling-block puzzle games.

mechanics.name: Linear Stage Gauntlet
mechanics.summary: Progression moves through a fixed sequence of confined stages without hub backtracking.
mechanics.problem_solved: Focused difficulty curve for corridor-style platformers.
anti_patterns: Avoid when hub-world-stage-gates is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Live Service Battle Pass
mechanics.summary: Seasonal tier rewards and rotating content drive ongoing engagement.
mechanics.problem_solved: Retention and monetization loops in live-service games.

mechanics.name: Live Service Events
mechanics.summary: Limited-time modes and rewards rotate on seasonal schedules.
mechanics.problem_solved: Ongoing engagement in service-based games.

mechanics.name: Loadout Customization
mechanics.summary: Pre-match gear and perk selection shapes combat identity and squad roles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player expression and meta strategy in competitive shooters.

mechanics.name: Local Multiplayer Competition
mechanics.summary: Same-screen or local wireless play pits players against each other in shared sessions.
mechanics.problem_solved: Social competitive play on handheld and couch setups.

mechanics.name: Location Based AR
mechanics.summary: Real-world GPS positions gate spawns, gyms, and events.
mechanics.problem_solved: Outdoor exploration hooks in mobile games.

mechanics.name: Lock Delay Placement
mechanics.summary: After a piece lands, a brief grace period allows final horizontal adjustments before it locks permanently.
mechanics.problem_solved: Forgiveness for last-moment corrections under high speed.

mechanics.name: Logic Puzzle Gating
mechanics.summary: Story progression requires solving self-contained logic puzzles at key locations.
mechanics.problem_solved: Brain-teaser pacing in puzzle-adventure hybrids.

mechanics.name: Loop Geometry Traversal
mechanics.summary: Level geometry includes curved loops and tunnels that the player traverses when carrying sufficient momentum.
mechanics.problem_solved: Showcases speed mechanics and creates iconic visual level design.

mechanics.name: Loot-Based Economy
mechanics.summary: Consumable resources such as health or ammunition are embedded within the game world as collectible pickups, either placed statically or dropped by enemies.
mechanics.problem_solved: Resource tension and reward for exploration and combat.
anti_patterns: Avoid when No Economy is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Machine Part Loot Crafting
mechanics.summary: Harvest components from robotic foes to craft gear and ammo.
mechanics.problem_solved: Combat-driven crafting loops in open-world action games.

mechanics.name: Magnetic Field Combat
mechanics.summary: Magnetic fields push, pull, or repel enemies and objects as a core combat and traversal tool.
mechanics.problem_solved: Novel interaction vocabulary for action platformers.

mechanics.name: Map-Resource Monster Groups
mechanics.summary: Neutral hostile groups occupy fixed map locations, offering experience points and item rewards when defeated, enabling optional power scaling.
mechanics.problem_solved: Optional difficulty tuning and resource farming before main challenges.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Empty Overworld is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Mask Transformation Abilities
mechanics.summary: Wearable masks grant unique movement, combat, and puzzle abilities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Ability variety without permanent class changes.

mechanics.name: Match Three Grid
mechanics.summary: Swap adjacent tiles to form lines of three or more matching pieces.
mechanics.problem_solved: Accessible puzzle loop with combo cascades.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Real-Time Action Combat is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Maze Chase High Score
mechanics.summary: The player navigates maze corridors collecting items while evading pursuers to maximize score.
mechanics.problem_solved: Simple readable loop for arcade maze games.

mechanics.name: Melee Primary Combat
mechanics.summary: The primary offensive loop uses close-range melee strikes rather than projectile weapons.
mechanics.problem_solved: Defines combat identity around spacing, timing, and positioning near enemies.
anti_patterns: Avoid when facing-locked-shooting is core to the experience
Avoid when decoupled-aim-control is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Metaprogression
mechanics.summary: A progression system that persists across multiple play sessions, allowing players to carry forward unlocks or structural changes that alter future runs.
mechanics.problem_solved: Long-term engagement and sense of permanent advancement.

mechanics.name: Microgame Rapid Challenges
mechanics.summary: Short timed minigames rotate rapidly, each with unique one-shot rules.
mechanics.problem_solved: Variety and comedy pacing in party microgame collections.

mechanics.name: Mid-Level Checkpoint
mechanics.summary: A save point within a stage resets the player to that position on death rather than restarting the entire level.
mechanics.problem_solved: Reduces frustration on longer stages while maintaining failure stakes.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Permadeath Run is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Minimalist Narrative Pacing
mechanics.summary: Sparse dialogue and environmental storytelling carry emotional arcs.
mechanics.problem_solved: Atmosphere-driven narrative without heavy exposition.

mechanics.name: Minimalist System Design
mechanics.summary: Elegant rules emerge from a few interacting systems with sparse UI.
mechanics.problem_solved: Depth from simplicity in indie puzzle and strategy games.

mechanics.name: Mirror World Expansion
mechanics.summary: A major story milestone unlocks a flipped, inverted, or mirrored version of the existing game world with new content.
mechanics.problem_solved: Doubles explorable content and reframes familiar spaces with new challenges.

mechanics.name: Mission Select Structure
mechanics.summary: A menu or hub lets the player choose missions or levels in flexible order with difficulty ratings.
mechanics.problem_solved: Replayability and player agency in mission-based games.

mechanics.name: Momentum-Based Speed
mechanics.summary: Character velocity accumulates from slopes, boosts, and sustained input, making speed a core traversal resource.
mechanics.problem_solved: Creates a distinct high-speed platforming feel and level design vocabulary.
anti_patterns: Avoid when precision-platforming is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Monster Capture Evolution
mechanics.summary: Wild creatures are caught, trained, and evolved through battle experience.
mechanics.problem_solved: Collection and growth loop defining monster RPG franchises.

mechanics.name: Mount Companion Control
mechanics.summary: The player rides or partners with a companion creature that adds movement, attack, or traversal abilities while mounted.
mechanics.problem_solved: Expands locomotion options and creates distinctive traversal identity.

mechanics.name: Multi-Line Scoring Multiplier
mechanics.summary: Clearing multiple lines simultaneously awards disproportionately higher scores than single-line clears.
mechanics.problem_solved: Incentivizes risky setup play for skilled players.

mechanics.name: Multi-Phase Boss Battle
mechanics.summary: A combat structure where boss encounters transition through discrete stages with distinct attack patterns, behaviors, or environmental conditions when health thresholds are reached.
mechanics.problem_solved: Sustains boss fight tension and teaches new patterns mid-encounter.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Single-Phase Boss is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Multi-Scenario Campaign
mechanics.summary: Linked scenario episodes share a persistent war map and roster across releases.
mechanics.problem_solved: Serialized tactics RPG storytelling across multiple parts.

mechanics.name: Musou Horde Combat
mechanics.summary: Fight hundreds of weak enemies with combo chains and special meters.
mechanics.problem_solved: Power fantasy crowd-control action in Warriors-style games.

mechanics.name: Narrative Chapter Progression
mechanics.summary: Story unfolds in discrete chapters with escalating mechanical complexity.
mechanics.problem_solved: Pacing narrative and difficulty together in indie platformers.

mechanics.name: Narrative Evidence Presentation
mechanics.summary: Collected clues are presented at key story beats to unlock new dialogue and areas.
mechanics.problem_solved: Inventory-as-narrative gating for detective and adventure games.

mechanics.name: Network Routing Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Connect stations with limited lines as passenger demand grows over time.
mechanics.problem_solved: Minimalist transit planning under escalating load.

mechanics.name: Next Piece Preview
mechanics.summary: A display shows the upcoming piece or pieces, enabling advance planning of placement strategy.
mechanics.problem_solved: Reduces randomness frustration and rewards strategic lookahead.

mechanics.name: Non-Linear Boss Selection
mechanics.summary: A progression structure in which players can freely choose the order of boss encounters, shaping their own path through the game.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player agency in route planning and ability acquisition order.

mechanics.name: Non Linear Notebook
mechanics.summary: Fill a journal freely as discoveries connect across non-sequential scenes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player-driven note-taking in mystery games.

mechanics.name: On-Rails Auto Scroll
mechanics.summary: The camera and player path move automatically through scripted combat sequences.
mechanics.problem_solved: Cinematic pacing in rail shooters without free movement burden.

mechanics.name: On Rails Photography
mechanics.summary: Rail-guided routes frame subjects for scoring-based photo challenges.
mechanics.problem_solved: Accessible photography gameplay on fixed camera paths.

mechanics.name: Open World Boss Gating
mechanics.summary: Major bosses and legacy dungeons gate progression across a seamless open map.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structured difficulty pacing in open-world action RPGs.

mechanics.name: Open World Exploration
mechanics.summary: Minimal UI encourages discovery of landmarks and secrets across vast terrain.
mechanics.problem_solved: Atmosphere-first traversal in open-world adventures.

games: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

mechanics.name: Open World Quest Log
mechanics.summary: A persistent world offers main and side quests tracked in a log with flexible completion order.
mechanics.problem_solved: Long-form exploration and player-driven pacing in open worlds.

games: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

mechanics.name: Open World Trading Economy
mechanics.summary: Buy low and sell high across star systems with dynamic commodity pricing.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player-driven economy loops in space exploration games.

mechanics.name: Optional Content Layering
mechanics.summary: Hidden rooms, passages, or content zones exist off main paths, requiring players to investigate suspicious environments.
mechanics.problem_solved: Depth and replay value for thorough explorers.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Linear Stage Gauntlet is core to the experience

games: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

mechanics.name: Over Shoulder Camera Combat
mechanics.summary: Close camera framing emphasizes weighty melee combos and cinematic encounters.
mechanics.problem_solved: Immersive third-person action in modern action-adventure games.

mechanics.name: Overworld Map Stage Selection
mechanics.summary: A hub map displays available stages as nodes or paths, letting the player choose which level to enter next.
mechanics.problem_solved: Gives route agency and structures non-linear progression visually.

mechanics.name: Overworld Side-Scroll
mechanics.summary: Side-view overworld movement connects discrete stages with power-up gates.
mechanics.problem_solved: Classic NES-era world map structure for platformers.

mechanics.name: Panic Button Burst
mechanics.summary: A unique offensive tool where the player emits a short-range radial burst that damages nearby enemies or destroys projectiles as an emergency option.
mechanics.problem_solved: Escape valve during overwhelming bullet patterns with resource cost.

mechanics.name: Parry Counter Attack
mechanics.summary: A timed defensive input deflects or negates an incoming attack and often enables a counter or resource reward.
mechanics.problem_solved: High-skill defensive option that rewards precise timing over passive dodging.

mechanics.name: Part Destruction Targeting
mechanics.summary: Enemies expose breakable parts that alter attacks when destroyed.
mechanics.problem_solved: Strategic targeting in action RPG and hunting games.

mechanics.name: Patterned Boss Encounter
mechanics.summary: A boss fight design in which the enemy executes a deterministic or semi-deterministic set of behaviors including telegraphed attacks and movement routines.
mechanics.problem_solved: Makes boss fights learnable and fair through readable patterns.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Random Attack Boss is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Permadeath Run Structure
mechanics.summary: Death ends the current run; meta-progression or knowledge carries between attempts.
mechanics.problem_solved: High stakes and run-based replay in roguelikes.
anti_patterns: Avoid when mid-level-checkpoint is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Permadeath Squad Tension
mechanics.summary: Soldier loss is permanent, making each tactical decision emotionally weighted.
mechanics.problem_solved: Stakes and attachment in squad-based tactics games.

mechanics.name: Permadeath Unit Loss
mechanics.summary: Fallen party members are permanently removed from the campaign roster.
mechanics.problem_solved: High-stakes decision-making in tactical RPGs.
anti_patterns: Avoid when mid-level-checkpoint is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Perspective Rotation Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Rotate the world to reveal hidden paths and align platforms.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spatial reasoning through camera or world twists.

mechanics.name: Physics Puzzle Gating
mechanics.summary: Portal gun or physics tools solve spatial puzzles to advance through test chambers.
mechanics.problem_solved: Environmental puzzle progression using simulated physics.

mechanics.name: Physics Ragdoll Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Floppy character bodies interact with physics objects to solve environmental puzzles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Comedic physics challenges in co-op puzzle platformers.

mechanics.name: Planet Gravity Platforming
mechanics.summary: Spherical or small planetoid stages apply local gravity, enabling upside-down traversal and curved platforming.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct spatial platforming identity beyond flat 3D stages.

mechanics.name: Point-and-Click Adventure
mechanics.summary: Cursor-driven exploration, inventory puzzles, and dialogue trees advance the plot.
mechanics.problem_solved: Classic PC adventure interaction model.

mechanics.name: Portal Spatial Reasoning
mechanics.summary: Linked portals enable non-Euclidean traversal and momentum puzzles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Novel spatial logic puzzles in first-person games.

mechanics.name: Possession Hat Mechanic
mechanics.summary: A throwable hat or capture tool binds to targets, granting control or special interactions with possessed entities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct capture-and-control loop for Mario Odyssey-style design.

mechanics.name: Power-Up Transformation States
mechanics.summary: Collectible items temporarily or persistently transform the player character, granting new movement or attack abilities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Variety and power scaling within levels without permanent progression gates.

mechanics.name: Precision Platforming
mechanics.summary: A character locomotion system in which movement responds immediately and exactly to player input, with no acceleration ramp-up, momentum carry, or analog smoothing.
mechanics.problem_solved: Snappy control for hazard navigation and accurate shooting during platforming.
anti_patterns: Avoid when input-aligned-movement is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Procedural Dungeon Generation
mechanics.summary: Floor layouts, enemy placement, and loot vary each run via procedural rules.
mechanics.problem_solved: Replayability and unpredictability in roguelike designs.

mechanics.name: Progress-Gated Dungeon Completion
mechanics.summary: The player must fully complete self-contained dungeons, each with unique challenges, in order to unlock new areas or abilities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structured pacing and milestone-based world expansion.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Open World From Start is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Projectile-Pattern Enemy Design
mechanics.summary: Enemies emit projectiles arranged in deterministic spatial formations such as spreads, spirals, rings, or waves that the player must navigate.
mechanics.problem_solved: Readable but escalating combat challenge in shooter contexts.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Unpatterned Spam is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Pseudo-3D Scroll Shooter
mechanics.summary: A behind-the-ship or into-the-screen perspective simulates depth while the player moves and fires in a scrolling 3D space.
mechanics.problem_solved: Delivers 3D shooter feel on limited hardware without full 3D engines.

mechanics.name: Purchase-Gated Upgrade
mechanics.summary: Players collect currency through gameplay and exchange it at vendors for permanent upgrades, creating economic progression choices.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player-driven build customization through spending decisions.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Free Upgrade Progression is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Puzzle Infiltration Combat
mechanics.summary: Transfer consciousness between robots to solve security puzzles and fight guards.
mechanics.problem_solved: Stealth-action hybrid with identity-swapping mechanics.

mechanics.name: Puzzle Room Exploration
mechanics.summary: Interconnected rooms hide switches, keys, and environmental secrets.
mechanics.problem_solved: Metroidvania-style gating in 3D adventure games.

games: The Return of the Obra Dinn

mechanics.name: Real-Time Action Combat
mechanics.summary: Direct player control over attacks, dodges, and combos in real time.
mechanics.problem_solved: Action feel in RPG contexts without turn-based pauses.

mechanics.name: Real Time Calendar Sync
mechanics.summary: In-game days and events advance with the real-world clock.
mechanics.problem_solved: Daily habit engagement in life simulation games.

mechanics.name: Real-Time Unit Command
mechanics.summary: Players select and direct multiple units simultaneously during live combat.
mechanics.problem_solved: Macro and micro management in real-time strategy games.

mechanics.name: Relationship Social System
mechanics.summary: NPC affinity grows through gifts, dialogue, and shared activities over time.
mechanics.problem_solved: Social progression and community building in life sims.

mechanics.name: Relic Passive Synergy
mechanics.summary: Passive relics or artifacts modify rules and combo with deck or combat systems.
mechanics.problem_solved: Build identity and discoverable synergies across a run.

mechanics.name: Remaster Collection Format
mechanics.summary: Multiple legacy titles are bundled with updated visuals and shared progression or menu wrappers.
mechanics.problem_solved: Preservation and accessibility of classic game libraries.

mechanics.name: Remaster Enhancement Mode
mechanics.summary: Modernized versions add quality-of-life features while preserving original level design.
mechanics.problem_solved: Faithful remakes that reduce friction for new audiences.

mechanics.name: Rescue Objective Stages
mechanics.summary: Stages require locating and extracting stranded units or miners before advancing to the next area.
mechanics.problem_solved: Varied objectives beyond pure enemy elimination in multi-stage shooters.

mechanics.name: Resource Chain Logistics
mechanics.summary: Production chains link raw materials to finished goods across a settlement.
mechanics.problem_solved: Supply web planning in builder and survival games.

mechanics.name: Resource-Gated Weapon
mechanics.summary: The character spends a resource such as ammo or energy to use special weapons, creating economy tension between primary and secondary attacks.
mechanics.problem_solved: Adds meaningful choice about when to spend limited offensive resources.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Unrestricted Primary Fire is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Resource Gathering Base Build
mechanics.summary: Harvest resources from the map to construct bases and produce military units.
mechanics.problem_solved: Economic layer underpinning RTS and strategy combat.

mechanics.name: Resource Scarcity Management
mechanics.summary: Limited ammo, healing, and save resources force careful risk assessment.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tension and dread in survival horror through constrained supplies.

mechanics.name: Rhythm Battle System
mechanics.summary: Combat inputs sync to music beats for bonus damage and combo chains.
mechanics.problem_solved: Audio-driven engagement in turn-based RPG battles.

mechanics.name: Rhythm Foot Panel
mechanics.summary: Step on directional panels in time with music for score and stamina challenges.
mechanics.problem_solved: Physical rhythm input using dedicated dance hardware.

mechanics.name: Rhythm Tap Timing
mechanics.summary: Notes scroll to a beat line; players tap, hold, or slide on precise timing windows.
mechanics.problem_solved: Music-driven skill expression in rhythm games.

mechanics.name: Roguelike Map Routing
mechanics.summary: Choose branching paths across a run map with risk-reward nodes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Run variety and player agency in roguelikes.

mechanics.name: Round-Based Health Duel
mechanics.summary: Matches are divided into rounds with separate health pools; winning a set number of rounds wins the match.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structured competitive pacing with comeback potential between rounds.

games: Street Fighter II: The World Warrior

mechanics.name: RPG Stat Progression
mechanics.summary: Defeating enemies or completing objectives grants experience that increases character stats such as HP, attack, or defense.
mechanics.problem_solved: Power scaling and build customization over a long campaign.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Fixed Stats is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Sandbox Assassination Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Open levels offer many scripted and emergent ways to eliminate targets.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player-authored solutions in stealth action games.

mechanics.name: Sandbox Goal Optional
mechanics.summary: Levels support completion goals but reward playful physics experimentation.
mechanics.problem_solved: Low-friction fun in co-op physics sandboxes.

mechanics.name: Score Chain Multiplier
mechanics.summary: Consecutive successful inputs build a multiplier that boosts final score.
mechanics.problem_solved: Risk-reward scoring escalation in arcade and rhythm games.

mechanics.name: Score Challenge Replay
mechanics.summary: Replay stages to beat high scores and unlock better ratings.
mechanics.problem_solved: Short-loop mastery in arcade-style experiences.

mechanics.name: Secret Exit Branching
mechanics.summary: Alternate level exits or hidden goals unlock parallel paths, bonus areas, or shortcuts on the world map.
mechanics.problem_solved: Rewards exploration and creates routing depth on overworld maps.

mechanics.name: Sector Clear Progression
mechanics.summary: Levels advance when a quota of enemies is destroyed or a sector boss is defeated before moving to the next zone.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structured pacing and clear short-term goals in arcade shooters.

mechanics.name: Shovel Dig Mechanics
mechanics.summary: Digging downward and bouncing on shovel blade extends platforming routes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Unique traversal hook for retro-inspired platformers.

mechanics.name: Skill Check Dialogue
mechanics.summary: Conversation options roll against character stats with pass/fail outcomes.
mechanics.problem_solved: RPG stats applied to narrative choices without combat.

mechanics.name: Smash Brothers Knockback
mechanics.summary: Damage increases launch force; KO by ring-out rather than health depletion alone.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct fighting game win condition focused on stage positioning.
anti_patterns: Avoid when round-based-health-duel is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Social Link Calendar
mechanics.summary: Daily schedules balance dungeon runs with relationship-building social events.
mechanics.problem_solved: Dual progression between combat and character bonds in modern JRPGs.

mechanics.name: Social Relationship Gifts
mechanics.summary: Build friendships through dialogue, gifts, and scheduled social events.
mechanics.problem_solved: Character bond progression in cozy life simulators.

mechanics.name: Social Relationship Meter
mechanics.summary: NPC affinity or friendship meters unlock dialogue, gifts, and story branches.
mechanics.problem_solved: Long-term character investment in life sims and RPGs.

mechanics.name: Soft Drop Control
mechanics.summary: Holding a direction accelerates piece descent without instant lock, allowing faster placement at a score or risk tradeoff.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player-controlled pacing between careful placement and speed.

mechanics.name: Song Stage Progression
mechanics.summary: Unlock and play through a curated track list with increasing difficulty tiers.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structured content pacing in rhythm titles.

mechanics.name: Special Infected Roles
mechanics.summary: Elite enemy types force unique team responses and coordination.
mechanics.problem_solved: Role-based PvE challenges in horde shooters.

mechanics.name: Special Move Command Input
mechanics.summary: Specific directional button sequences executed within a time window trigger character-specific special attacks.
mechanics.problem_solved: Skill expression through input execution and character differentiation.

games: Street Fighter II: The World Warrior

mechanics.name: Spell Resource Meter
mechanics.summary: A dedicated meter limits spell or special ability usage, refilled through combat or rest.
mechanics.problem_solved: Resource tension for powerful abilities without unlimited spam.

mechanics.name: Spin Attack Combat
mechanics.summary: A rolling or spinning offensive state destroys enemies on contact and may be required for certain interactions.
mechanics.problem_solved: Offensive option that synergizes with high-speed movement.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Ranged-Only Combat is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Squad Drop Deployment
mechanics.summary: Teams choose drop zones and parachute in to begin a match.
mechanics.problem_solved: Opening strategy and loot route planning in battle royale.

mechanics.name: Stamina Resource Management
mechanics.summary: Grip, breath, or stamina meters limit sustained climbing and sprinting.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tension during extended physical challenges.

mechanics.name: Stealth Detection Loop
mechanics.summary: Enemy awareness meters, vision cones, or alert phases govern when combat or failure triggers.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tension and planning in stealth-action gameplay.

mechanics.name: Stealth Platforming Hybrid
mechanics.summary: Levels alternate between platforming traversal and stealth avoidance of detection zones.
mechanics.problem_solved: Genre blend for varied pacing in action-adventure platformers.

games: Sly Cooper and the Thievius Raccoonus

mechanics.name: Stereoscopic Depth Platforming
mechanics.summary: 3D depth perception on autostereoscopic displays informs jump distance and enemy positioning.
mechanics.problem_solved: Handheld 3D hardware used as a gameplay readability aid.

mechanics.name: Story Battle Ladder
mechanics.summary: Progress through a scripted series of duels tied to narrative arcs.
mechanics.problem_solved: Single-player structure in fighting games.

mechanics.name: Super Meter Resource
mechanics.summary: A meter fills through successful offense or defense and unlocks powerful super or ultimate attacks when full.
mechanics.problem_solved: Comeback potential and reward for aggressive or skilled play.

mechanics.name: Swipe Note Battle System
mechanics.summary: Touch or button swipes match on-screen prompts during music-driven combat.
mechanics.problem_solved: Rhythm-action hybrid combat in handheld JRPGs.

mechanics.name: Tactical Loot Scavenge
mechanics.summary: Gear up from world spawns and defeated players under time pressure.
mechanics.problem_solved: Risk-reward equipment progression in extraction shooters and BR.

mechanics.name: Tactics Grid Movement
mechanics.summary: Units move on a discrete grid with action points, facing, and terrain modifiers.
mechanics.problem_solved: Structured tactical positioning and turn order in SRPGs.

mechanics.name: Tech Tree Unlocks
mechanics.summary: Research or building prerequisites gate access to advanced units and abilities.
mechanics.problem_solved: Long-term strategic progression in strategy and RTS titles.

mechanics.name: Telegraphed Threat Loop
mechanics.summary: A combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.
mechanics.problem_solved: Gives players time to react and learn attack timing.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Random Bullet Hell is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Territory Paint Shooter
mechanics.summary: Cover terrain in team ink to control space and secure objectives.
mechanics.problem_solved: Non-lethal team shooter objectives with unique movement.

mechanics.name: Tetromino Rotation
mechanics.summary: Falling polyomino pieces rotate in fixed increments to fit into the playfield grid.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spatial puzzle depth through orientation choice under time pressure.

mechanics.name: Theme Park Economy
mechanics.summary: Balance ride placement, pricing, and guest happiness for profit.
mechanics.problem_solved: Creative business sim loops in theme park builders.

mechanics.name: Time-Charged Attack
mechanics.summary: The character's attack execution can be delayed by holding an input, converting charge time into increased effect upon release.
mechanics.problem_solved: Adds depth to primary fire through risk-reward timing.
anti_patterns: Avoid when instant-fire-attack is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Time-Limited Objectives
mechanics.summary: A countdown clock forces prioritization of quests and dungeon routes.
mechanics.problem_solved: Urgency and scheduling tension in time-loop adventures.

mechanics.name: Time Piece Objectives
mechanics.summary: Stages contain multiple optional objectives that award time pieces or tokens toward hub unlocks.
mechanics.problem_solved: Mario 64-style optional goals within open 3D stages.

mechanics.name: Time Travel Narrative
mechanics.summary: Story branches across eras with actions in the past altering the present world state.
mechanics.problem_solved: Non-linear storytelling tied to world-state changes.

mechanics.name: Tongue Grapple Traversal
mechanics.summary: An extendable tongue or grapple pulls the character toward targets or swings them across gaps.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct mobility hook for character-driven platformers.

mechanics.name: Touchscreen Drawing Mechanics
mechanics.summary: Draw symbols or paths on a touch surface to cast spells or solve puzzles.
mechanics.problem_solved: Hardware-specific interaction in handheld RPGs.

mechanics.name: Track Design Sandbox
mechanics.summary: Sculpt roller coaster layouts with banking, drops, and test runs.
mechanics.problem_solved: Player expression in construction-focused sims.

mechanics.name: Train Routing Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Lay track and schedule trains to deliver goods across a logistics network.
mechanics.problem_solved: Spatial planning under throughput constraints in transport puzzles.

mechanics.name: Tube Lane Rotation
mechanics.summary: The player rotates around a tubular playfield, moving between lanes while enemies advance from the far end.
mechanics.problem_solved: Creates spatial shooter depth with readable lane-based threat vectors.

mechanics.name: Turn-Based Grid Strategy
mechanics.summary: Players take alternating turns moving units on a grid with terrain and range rules.
mechanics.problem_solved: Deliberate tactical decision-making in strategy games.

mechanics.name: Turn-Based Party Combat
mechanics.summary: Battles resolve in turns with a party of characters selecting commands from menus.
mechanics.problem_solved: Tactical depth and party build expression in RPGs.
anti_patterns: Avoid when Real-Time Action Combat is core to the experience

mechanics.name: Type Matchup Combat
mechanics.summary: Element or type charts create strong and weak matchups between combatants.
mechanics.problem_solved: Strategic team building and move selection in creature RPGs.

mechanics.name: Unit Assignment Puzzle
mechanics.summary: Assign abilities to autonomous units to navigate hazards toward an exit.
mechanics.problem_solved: Indirect control puzzle design in Lemmings-style games.

mechanics.name: Unrestricted Primary Fire
mechanics.summary: A default attack scheme in which the main weapon operates outside any economy, firing freely on input without depleting inventories, meters, or charges.
mechanics.problem_solved: Ensures the player always has a baseline offensive option during platforming.

mechanics.name: Vacuum Ghost Combat
mechanics.summary: Suck up spirits with a poltergust while managing light and darkness.
mechanics.problem_solved: Unique combat identity in haunted house adventures.

mechanics.name: Vehicle Physics Racing
mechanics.summary: Weighty vehicles with drift, boost, and terrain interaction.
mechanics.problem_solved: Kinetic racing feel beyond arcade lap timers.

mechanics.name: Vehicle Tuning Economy
mechanics.summary: Currency earned from races funds parts and tuning upgrades that modify vehicle performance.
mechanics.problem_solved: Long-term progression and build customization in racing games.

mechanics.name: Vine Rope Climbing
mechanics.summary: The player climbs and descends vertical ropes, vines, or chains as a primary traversal method.
mechanics.problem_solved: Enables vertical arcade stage design distinct from horizontal platforming.

mechanics.name: Virtual Pet Care
mechanics.summary: Feed, train, and play with digital pets on daily schedules.
mechanics.problem_solved: Low-pressure nurturing loops on handheld devices.

mechanics.name: Wall Merge Traversal
mechanics.summary: The player flattens into 2D to slip through wall cracks and reach hidden areas.
mechanics.problem_solved: Distinct spatial puzzle mechanic for top-down Zelda-style games.

mechanics.name: Warp Escape Ability
mechanics.summary: A limited-use warp or blink relocates the player instantly to escape overwhelming enemy formations.
mechanics.problem_solved: Emergency repositioning tool in dense bullet patterns.

mechanics.name: Weapon Triangle Tactics
mechanics.summary: Sword, lance, and axe (or equivalent) form a circular advantage/disadvantage loop.
mechanics.problem_solved: Positioning and class counterplay in tactical RPGs.
